content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://cjil.uchicago.edu/print-archive/digital-services-act-and-eu-global-regulator-internet", "source_title": "The Digital Services Act and the EU as the Global Regulator of the ...", "source_type": "web", "source_quality": 0.7, "overall_relevance": 0.65, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "In 2016, the EU agreed with Facebook, Microsoft, Twitter, and YouTube on a code of conduct countering hate speech online.", "direct_quote": "In 2016, the EU agreed with Facebook, Microsoft, Twitter, and YouTube on a code of conduct countering hate speech online.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "As part of this code, the platforms agreed to rules or Community Guidelines and to practice content moderation in conformity with them.", "direct_quote": "As part of this code, the platforms agreed to rules or Community Guidelines and to practice content moderation in conformity with them.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "Similarly, widespread misinformation during the COVID-19 pandemic and the perceptions of these platforms\u2019 inadequate responses led the Europ...", "direct_quote": "Similarly, widespread misinformation during the COVID-19 pandemic and the perceptions of these platforms\u2019 inadequate responses led the European Union (EU) to pass the 2022 Digital Services Act (DSA) to combat misinformation and extremist speech.1\nDig", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}], "evidence_summary": "Auto-extracted 3 facts."}]}
